transport: UDP 546 (client) / 547 (server, relay); client multicast to ff02::1:2 (All_DHCP_Relay_Agents_and_Servers)
purpose: DHCPv6 configures IPv6 hosts with addresses, prefixes (Prefix Delegation), and other parameters. It runs in two modes selected by the Router Advertisement M/O flags: stateful (M=1, the server assigns addresses in an IA_NA) and stateless (O=1, SLAAC provides the address while DHCPv6 supplies options such as DNS). The client/server exchange is Solicit -> Advertise -> Request -> Reply, with Renew/Rebind to extend leases. Relay agents (RELAY-FORW/RELAY-REPL) carry messages across links when the server is off-link. Prefix Delegation (IA_PD) hands a routable prefix to a downstream router.

states.name: SOLICIT
states.description: Client multicasts Solicit to locate DHCPv6 servers; no lease yet.

states.name: REQUESTING
states.description: Client received one or more Advertise messages, selected a server, and sent Request for the offered addresses/prefixes.

states.name: BOUND
states.description: Client received Reply and has valid address(es)/prefix(es) with T1/T2 timers running. Steady operational state.

states.name: RENEWING
states.description: T1 elapsed; client unicasts Renew to the leasing server to extend lifetimes.

states.name: REBINDING
states.description: T2 elapsed without a Renew reply; client multicasts Rebind to any server to extend lifetimes.

states.name: NO-BINDING
states.description: Solicit/Request never yields a usable Reply (no server, relay broken, or NoAddrsAvail) — the client has no DHCPv6 address/prefix.

transitions.trigger: One or more Advertise messages received; best server selected.
No Advertise received after SOL_MAX_RT backoff (no server/relay reachable).
Reply carries NoAddrsAvail/NoPrefixAvail or no Reply arrives.
T2 elapsed with no Renew reply.
Valid lifetime expires with no Reply; binding lost.

timers.name: T1 (Renew time)
timers.description: When the client begins renewing its lease with the original server.
timers.on_expiry: Client enters RENEWING and unicasts Renew.
timers.tuning_advice: Server-controlled; lower for tighter lease control.

timers.name: T2 (Rebind time)
timers.description: When the client begins rebinding with any server if Renew failed.
timers.on_expiry: Client enters REBINDING and multicasts Rebind.

timers.name: SOL_MAX_RT
timers.description: Maximum Solicit retransmission interval; bounds how often a client with no server keeps soliciting.
timers.tuning_advice: Rarely tuned; server may override via option.

messages.name: SOLICIT (type 1)
messages.when_sent: Client multicasts to ff02::1:2 at startup to find servers.

messages.name: ADVERTISE (type 2)
messages.when_sent: Server responds to a Solicit indicating availability.

messages.name: REQUEST (type 3)
messages.description: Client requests configuration from a chosen server.
messages.when_sent: Client unicasts after selecting an Advertise.

messages.name: REPLY (type 7)
messages.when_sent: Server answers Request/Renew/Rebind/Information-Request (or Solicit with Rapid Commit).
key_fields: assigned IA_NA/IA_PD with valid/preferred lifetimes, T1/T2

messages.name: RENEW (type 5) / REBIND (type 6)
messages.when_sent: Renew unicast at T1 to the leasing server; Rebind multicast at T2 to any server.

messages.name: INFORMATION-REQUEST (type 11)
messages.when_sent: Client with O=1 (and M=0) requests only options such as DNS.

messages.name: RELAY-FORW (type 12) / RELAY-REPL (type 13)
messages.when_sent: Relay wraps client messages toward the server (FORW) and unwraps server replies toward the client (REPL).

messages.name: DECLINE (type 9)
messages.when_sent: Client found the DHCPv6-assigned address already in use (DAD failed).

dependencies.name: Router Advertisement M/O flags
dependencies.description: Hosts only run stateful DHCPv6 when the RA M flag is set, and only fetch stateless options when the O flag is set. Wrong flags mean clients never start DHCPv6 (see IPv6 ND).

dependencies.name: DHCPv6 relay agent
dependencies.description: When the server is off-link, a relay must forward Solicit/Request (RELAY-FORW) and return RELAY-REPL to the client. Misconfigured or one-way relays are a top failure cause.

dependencies.name: UDP 546/547 + multicast reachability
dependencies.description: Client uses UDP 546, server/relay UDP 547, and client multicast ff02::1:2. ACLs blocking these or pruned multicast break DHCPv6.

dependencies.name: ICMPv6 / DAD
dependencies.description: A client runs DAD on a DHCPv6-assigned address and will DECLINE it if a duplicate is detected, looping the assignment.

notes.note: Relay with 'ipv6 dhcp relay address <server>' per interface; inspect 'show ipv6 dhcp relay' and bindings. NX-OS is usually a relay, not the server.

Server pools via 'ipv6 dhcp pool'; relay via 'ipv6 dhcp relay destination'. 'show ipv6 dhcp binding', 'show ipv6 dhcp interface', 'debug ipv6 dhcp' are the core tools. Prefix delegation via 'prefix-delegation pool'.
DHCPv6 proxy/server/relay configured under 'dhcp ipv6'; verify with 'show dhcp ipv6 relay binding' / 'show dhcp ipv6 proxy binding' and relay statistics.

SONiC acts as a DHCPv6 relay via the dhcp_relay container (dhcp6relay), driven by CONFIG_DB DHCP_RELAY entries. Inspect 'redis-cli -n 4 KEYS "DHCP_RELAY*"' and 'docker logs dhcp_relay'. No on-box DHCPv6 server.

sources.title: RFC 8415 — Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol for IPv6 (DHCPv6) [Mrugalski et al., 2018]
sources.verifying_quote: Clients and servers exchange DHCP messages using UDP. The client uses a link-local address or addresses determined through other mechanisms for transmitting and receiving DHCP messages.

sources.title: Internal KB D5 — DHCP and DHCPv6 (kb/networking/ip-addressing/dhcp-and-dhcpv6.json)
sources.verifying_quote: Identity Association (IA) containers hold address info: IA_NA (non-temporary), IA_TA (temporary), IA_PD (prefix delegation for downstream networks).

failure_modes.scenario: Client gets no DHCPv6 address — Solicit unanswered (no Advertise/Reply)
failure_modes.description: The client multicasts Solicit but never receives Advertise/Reply, so it stays with a link-local only. Either no server/relay is reachable, the RA M flag is not set (so the host never starts stateful DHCPv6), or UDP 546/547/multicast is filtered.
symptoms: Host has only a link-local (or a SLAAC address) and no DHCPv6-assigned address
DHCPv6 server/relay shows no Solicit received from the segment
Client repeatedly retransmits Solicit (backing off to SOL_MAX_RT)

root_causes.cause: RA M flag is 0, so hosts never initiate stateful DHCPv6 (they SLAAC instead)
No DHCPv6 relay configured on the client's gateway and the server is off-link
ACL/firewall blocking UDP 546/547 or the ff02::1:2 multicast
Server out of addresses (NoAddrsAvail) or wrong pool bound to the link

resolutions.action: Confirm the RA M flag is set on the client's segment (otherwise hosts won't do stateful DHCPv6) — see ipv6-nd RA failure modes
Confirm the RA M flag is set on the client's segment (otherwise hosts won't do stateful DHCPv6) — see ipv6-nd RA failure modes
Confirm the RA M flag is set on the client's segment (otherwise hosts won't do stateful DHCPv6) — see ipv6-nd RA failure modes
Verify a relay is configured pointing at the server, or the server is on-link
Verify a relay is configured pointing at the server, or the server is on-link
Verify a relay is configured pointing at the server, or the server is on-link
Permit UDP 546/547 and ff02::1:2 multicast end-to-end
Permit UDP 546/547 and ff02::1:2 multicast end-to-end
Permit UDP 546/547 and ff02::1:2 multicast end-to-end
sonic: confirm DHCP_RELAY is configured for the VLAN and the dhcp_relay container is running: 'redis-cli -n 4 KEYS "DHCP_RELAY*"' and 'docker ps | grep dhcp_relay'

verify_commands.confirms_if: Relay not configured/forwarding confirms why Solicit is unanswered
verify_commands.look_for: relay destination(s) and per-interface relay state

verify_commands.confirms_if: Interface DHCPv6 role confirms relay/server is active
verify_commands.look_for: interface in relay or server mode with the right destination

verify_commands.confirms_if: No binding for the client confirms it never completed
verify_commands.look_for: a binding for the client's DUID; absence = no Reply issued

verify_commands.confirms_if: No relay binding confirms Solicit/Request not relayed
verify_commands.look_for: relay bindings for the segment

verify_commands.confirms_if: Missing DHCP_RELAY config confirms relay not set up
verify_commands.look_for: DHCP_RELAY entries for the client's VLAN

verify_commands.confirms_if: Relay logs show whether Solicit is seen/forwarded
verify_commands.look_for: Solicit received and RELAY-FORW sent to the server

failure_modes.scenario: Relay forwards Solicit but RELAY-REPLY is dropped — client never gets the Reply
failure_modes.description: The relay sends RELAY-FORW and the server answers with RELAY-REPL that reaches the relay, but the relay does not deliver the unwrapped Reply to the client. A packet capture on the relay shows the RELAY-REPL arriving and then disappearing. This matches Cisco bug CSCvs22896 on Catalyst 9500 (16.11.1 / 16.12.x) for Information-Request relay-reply, and also occurs with relay interface/VLAN misbinding.
symptoms: Capture on the relay shows DHCPv6 RELAY-REPL arriving from the server
Client never receives the Reply and keeps retransmitting
Server logs show it answered, but the client side stays unconfigured

root_causes.cause: Relay software defect dropping RELAY-REPL (e.g. IOS-XE CSCvs22896 on Cat9500 16.11.1/16.12.1-16.12.3a)
Relay output interface/VLAN for the client not correctly bound, so the unwrapped Reply is sent on the wrong interface
Interface-Id/link-address mismatch so the relay cannot map the Reply back to the client link
Asymmetric path / uRPF dropping the server-sourced RELAY-REPL at the relay

resolutions.action: Capture on the relay to confirm RELAY-REPL ingress and whether it is re-emitted toward the client
Capture on the relay to confirm RELAY-REPL ingress and whether it is re-emitted toward the client
Capture on the relay to confirm RELAY-REPL ingress and whether it is re-emitted toward the client
Verify the relay's client-facing interface and the relay destination/source addressing
Verify the relay's client-facing interface and the relay destination/source addressing
Verify the relay's client-facing interface and the relay destination/source addressing
iosxe: if running an affected Cat9500 release for CSCvs22896, upgrade past the fixed release (no workaround documented)
sonic: inspect 'docker logs dhcp_relay' for RELAY-REPL handling and confirm the client-facing VLAN binding in DHCP_RELAY

verify_commands.confirms_if: Relay binding present but client unconfigured points to a reply-delivery drop
verify_commands.look_for: relay binding state for the client link

verify_commands.confirms_if: Confirms relay has the binding while client gets nothing
verify_commands.look_for: binding for the client; reply-side delivery is the gap

verify_commands.confirms_if: Live relay trace shows RELAY-REPL received and forwarding result
verify_commands.look_for: RELAY-REPL received and whether a Reply is sent to the client

verify_commands.confirms_if: Counters show received vs forwarded replies
verify_commands.look_for: relay-reply received vs transmitted counts (a gap = dropped replies)

verify_commands.confirms_if: Relay log shows reply received but not delivered
verify_commands.look_for: RELAY-REPL/Reply handling and any error delivering to the client VLAN

failure_modes.scenario: IA_PD prefix not delegated or not routed to the downstream network
failure_modes.description: A requesting router asks for a delegated prefix (IA_PD) but either receives none (NoPrefixAvail / pool exhausted / PD not configured) or receives one that is not installed as a route toward the downstream, so downstream hosts have no connectivity even though the WAN side is up.
symptoms: Requesting router has a WAN/uplink address but no delegated prefix on the LAN side
Downstream hosts get no global prefix to SLAAC from
Server shows NoPrefixAvail or no IA_PD in the Reply
Delegated prefix present on the router but no route toward the LAN

root_causes.cause: DHCPv6 server/delegating router has no PD pool or it is exhausted
Requesting router not configured to request IA_PD (only IA_NA requested)
Delegated prefix not redistributed/installed as a route toward the downstream interface
Relay in the path not configured to relay PD, or PD route not added on the delegating side

resolutions.action: Confirm the delegating side has a PD pool with available prefixes
Confirm the delegating side has a PD pool with available prefixes
Confirm the delegating side has a PD pool with available prefixes
Verify the requesting router requests IA_PD and binds the prefix to the LAN interface
Verify the requesting router requests IA_PD and binds the prefix to the LAN interface
Verify the requesting router requests IA_PD and binds the prefix to the LAN interface
Ensure the delegated prefix is installed/redistributed as a route toward the downstream
Ensure the delegated prefix is installed/redistributed as a route toward the downstream
Ensure the delegated prefix is installed/redistributed as a route toward the downstream

verify_commands.confirms_if: Presence/absence of an IA_PD binding confirms delegation status
verify_commands.look_for: IA_PD prefix in the binding vs only IA_NA

verify_commands.confirms_if: Pool prefix exhaustion/misconfig confirms why PD is not delegated
verify_commands.look_for: PD prefix pool size and current utilization

verify_commands.confirms_if: Missing route for the delegated prefix confirms a routing gap
verify_commands.look_for: a route covering the delegated prefix toward the LAN

verify_commands.confirms_if: Confirms delegated-prefix binding on IOS-XR
verify_commands.look_for: IA_PD prefix bound for the client

verify_commands.confirms_if: Relay PD handling confirms whether PD is carried in the path
verify_commands.look_for: relay configured to forward DHCPv6 including PD

failure_modes.scenario: M/O flag mismatch — clients use SLAAC vs DHCPv6 unexpectedly
failure_modes.description: Address-source selection is driven entirely by the RA M/O flags. If operators expect DHCPv6 addressing but the RA has M=0, hosts SLAAC and never ask DHCPv6; if they expect SLAAC but M=1, hosts wait on stateful DHCPv6. The DHCPv6 server may be perfectly healthy while no client uses it.
symptoms: DHCPv6 server shows no/low Solicit volume despite many hosts
Hosts have SLAAC addresses when DHCPv6 assignment was intended (or vice versa)
Mixed behavior across vendors/OSes on the same VLAN

root_causes.cause: RA M flag not set, so hosts never start stateful DHCPv6
RA O flag not set, so hosts never fetch stateless options (DNS) via DHCPv6
Prefix advertised with the Autonomous (A) flag, so hosts SLAAC even when DHCPv6 was intended
Inconsistent RA flags across redundant routers on the same segment

resolutions.action: Decide the addressing model and set RA flags to match: M=1 for stateful DHCPv6, O=1 for stateless options, clear A on the prefix to force DHCPv6-only addressing
Decide the addressing model and set RA flags to match: M=1 for stateful DHCPv6, O=1 for stateless options, clear A on the prefix to force DHCPv6-only addressing
Decide the addressing model and set RA flags to match: M=1 for stateful DHCPv6, O=1 for stateless options, clear A on the prefix to force DHCPv6-only addressing
Make RA flags consistent across all routers on the segment
Make RA flags consistent across all routers on the segment
Make RA flags consistent across all routers on the segment

verify_commands.confirms_if: RA M/O/A flag values confirm the actual address-selection policy
verify_commands.look_for: Managed/Other config flags and prefix Autonomous flag in the RA

verify_commands.confirms_if: RA flag values confirm SLAAC-vs-DHCPv6 policy
verify_commands.look_for: 'Hosts use ... DHCP' / managed-config-flag and other-config-flag

verify_commands.confirms_if: Advertised flags confirm the addressing model in effect
verify_commands.look_for: M/O flags and prefix A/L flags in the RA

verify_commands.confirms_if: Default route + prefix source hints at which mode hosts followed
verify_commands.look_for: SLAAC prefix vs reliance on DHCPv6

failure_modes.scenario: Client DECLINEs the DHCPv6 address (DAD failure) — assignment loops
failure_modes.description: After the server assigns an address, the client runs DAD on it. If a duplicate is detected (another node already uses it, often a stale lease or an overlapping static address), the client sends DECLINE and asks again, looping the assignment and leaving the host without a stable address.
symptoms: Server logs repeated Decline messages for assigned addresses
Client cycles through addresses without settling
Duplicate-address / DAD syslog on the client for the assigned address

root_causes.cause: Server pool overlaps a statically configured address on the segment
Stale server bindings handing out an address still in use elsewhere
Two DHCPv6 servers with overlapping pools on the same link
L2 loop reflecting the client's DAD NS, making every address look duplicate

resolutions.action: Reconcile the server pool against static assignments and remove overlaps
Reconcile the server pool against static assignments and remove overlaps
Reconcile the server pool against static assignments and remove overlaps
Clear stale bindings on the server and ensure a single authoritative pool per link
Clear stale bindings on the server and ensure a single authoritative pool per link
Clear stale bindings on the server and ensure a single authoritative pool per link
Rule out a L2 loop reflecting DAD before blaming the pool (see ipv6-nd DAD failure)
Rule out a L2 loop reflecting DAD before blaming the pool (see ipv6-nd DAD failure)
Rule out a L2 loop reflecting DAD before blaming the pool (see ipv6-nd DAD failure)

verify_commands.confirms_if: Bindings overlapping a static/duplicate address confirm the cause
verify_commands.look_for: assigned addresses vs known static addresses on the segment

verify_commands.confirms_if: Live trace shows Decline and re-Solicit looping
verify_commands.look_for: Decline messages and the address being rejected

verify_commands.confirms_if: Binding churn confirms repeated decline/reassign
verify_commands.look_for: rapidly changing bindings for the same client

verify_commands.confirms_if: Relay sees the client repeatedly restarting the exchange
verify_commands.look_for: binding state cycling for the client
